topic: In China employee termination: decision-tree execution model for foreign employers

sources.title: Labor Contract Law of the People's Republic of China
Social Insurance Law of the People's Republic of China
People's Republic of China - Individual taxes on personal income
Key Recent Developments in China's Employment Law: A China-U.S. Comparative Perspective for Multinational Employers

consensus: Termination compliance for foreign employers is primarily an execution-control problem under deadline pressure.
A documented decision path for in-cycle vs off-cycle release lowers correction cost and dispute risk.
HR, payroll, manager, and vendor roles must be explicit at each decision gate.
uncertainties: Local city-level practice can differ in edge cases and evidentiary expectations.
Certain component-level treatment details may require local confirmation before release.
English summaries are practical references but may not capture all local implementation nuances.
